title: ceraia clavator
side_effects: Ceraia clavator can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive problems. Ingesting large quantities of the plant's extract can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Overuse of the plant's essential oils can cause respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma attacks, particularly in individuals with pre-existing conditions. Improper handling of the plant can also cause skin contact dermatitis and eye irritation.
